# src/quality/inconsistency

Detects and reports documentation drift and code duplication across the codebase by comparing TypeScript/JavaScript exports against .sum file documentation and identifying duplicate symbols exported from multiple files.

## Contents

**[code-vs-doc.ts](./code-vs-doc.ts)** — Extracts exported identifiers via regex `/^[ \t]*export\s+(?:default\s+)?(?:function|class|const|let|var|type|interface|enum)\s+(\w+)/gm` and compares against .sum file text using case-sensitive substring matching; returns `CodeDocInconsistency` when exports are missing from documentation.

**[code-vs-code.ts](./code-vs-code.ts)** — Builds Map<string, string[]> of export names to file paths and flags duplicate exports with `CodeCodeInconsistency` type='code-vs-code', severity='warning', pattern='duplicate-export'; requires caller to scope input to per-directory groups.

**[reporter.ts](./reporter.ts)** — Aggregates inconsistency arrays into `InconsistencyReport` with typed counts (codeVsDoc, codeVsCode, phantomPaths, errors, warnings, info) and renders plain text (`[ERROR]`/`[WARN]`/`[INFO]` prefixes) or GitHub-flavored Markdown table formats.

## Inconsistency Detection Pipeline

`code-vs-doc.checkCodeVsDoc` → compares source exports against .sum content → returns `CodeDocInconsistency` with `missingFromDoc` array.

`code-vs-code.checkCodeVsCode` → detects duplicate exports across file array → returns `CodeCodeInconsistency[]` with `files` array for each duplicate symbol.

`reporter.buildInconsistencyReport` → aggregates typed issues and metadata (projectRoot, filesChecked, durationMs) → `InconsistencyReport`.

`reporter.formatReportForCli` / `reporter.formatReportAsMarkdown` → renders structured reports for terminal or PR comments.

## Behavioral Contracts

**Export Extraction Pattern:** `/^[ \t]*export\s+(?:default\s+)?(?:function|class|const|let|var|type|interface|enum)\s+(\w+)/gm` (code-vs-doc.ts `extractExports`) — captures identifiers from export declarations, ignores re-exports and comments.

**CLI Severity Tags:** `[ERROR]`, `[WARN]`, `[INFO]` (reporter.ts `formatReportForCli`) — prefix format for terminal output.

**Markdown Table Format:** Pipe-delimited with columns `Severity | Type | Description | Location` (reporter.ts `formatReportAsMarkdown`) — escapes backticks in paths.

## Type Surface

**CodeDocInconsistency** (from ../types.js) — `{ type: 'code-vs-doc', filePath: string, severity: 'error'|'warning'|'info', missingFromDoc: string[], missingFromCode: string[], message: string }`.

**CodeCodeInconsistency** (from ../types.js) — `{ type: 'code-vs-code', files: string[], severity: 'warning', pattern: 'duplicate-export', exportName: string, message: string }`.

**InconsistencyReport** (from ../types.js) — `{ summary: { total, codeVsDoc, codeVsCode, phantomPaths, errors, warnings, info }, issues: Inconsistency[], metadata: { timestamp, projectRoot, filesChecked, durationMs } }`.

## File Relationships

`code-vs-doc.extractExports` is imported by `code-vs-code.checkCodeVsCode` for shared symbol extraction logic; both checkers export distinct inconsistency types that `reporter.buildInconsistencyReport` aggregates into a unified report structure.
